title: An Observational Study of Afatinib 30 mg Daily in Patients With Advanced Non-Small-Cell Lung Cancer Harboring Common EGFR Mutations Treated With Afatinib.
abstract: Afatinib is an approved first-line treatment for advanced non-small-cell lung cancer (NSCLC) harboring sensitizing EGFR mutations. At the standard 40 mg dose, 28%-53% of patients in randomized trials required dose reductions due to adverse events (AEs), predominantly cutaneous and gastrointestinal. This study evaluated whether a 30 mg starting dose could improve tolerability without compromising efficacy.
